programmes.show_description: Father-of-three Chris undergoes high risk brain surgery and mental health nurse Kate is called into a police station to treat a confused woman at risk of self-harm.
Louis Theroux heads to America to investigate one of the most controversial and captivating icons of recent times: gun-toting, self-described 'gay hillbilly' and 'Tiger King' Joe Exotic.
Three contestants try to answer their way to a pile of life-changing cash, with a little help from some famous faces including Myleene Klass, Prue Leith and Stephen Mangan.
Shopping from Home.
The Mouthtrap: Murder most foul in the quirky ratings topper. One of Ben's patients, while under anaesthetic, admits to killing his wife. And so naturally, Susan turns sleuth.
While You Weren't Sleeping: The popular domestic sitcom starring Robert Lindsay and Zoe Wanamaker. There are problems in the bedroom which threaten to drive Ben and Susan apart.
Dentist to the Stars: Robert Lindsay and Zoe Wanamaker star in the family sitcom. One of Britain's best-loved heart throbs visits Ben's surgery. Peter Capaldi guest stars.
A Wife Less Ordinary: Comedy from the bonkers Harpers. Susan, bored with her lot, opts to run her life as a single girl again. Janey takes the opportunity to try running the home.
The Book of Love: Domestic sitcom with a great cast. Susan attends a neighbour's book club. Michael runs a booking agency exclusively for Roger, while Ben books a seat on the sofa.
Going Dental: Susan forsakes Ben's dental conference to write her novel. Ben seeks a new partnership in Harley Street but ends up with more partners than he had bargained for.
Ben Shephard hosts the quiz show in which four players take on an extraordinary machine.
Ben Shephard hosts the quiz show in which four players take on an extraordinary machine.
Ben Shephard hosts the quiz show in which four players take on an extraordinary machine.
Marlee, Jethro & Muffin: Tea-drinking Bedlington Terrier Muffin has a fractured leg due to her limb deformity, and Labrador Jethro's arthritic elbows are stopping him from walking properly.
Lexie, Charlie & Locky: Maine Coon Lexie needs a bilateral total hip replacement, while bouncy Border Terrier Charlie is struggling to walk and dwarf rabbit Locky has broken his leg.
Oz, Ollie & Bayleigh: Labrador puppy Ollie is rushed in after being hit by a car, while Bullmastiff Bayleigh has a painful lump near her ankle and adventurous cat Oz's leg needs saving.
Merida, Tito & Monti: Rough Collie puppy Merida has deformed front legs, fidgety Cockapoo Monti has fractured his knee and Weimaraner Tito is brought in after being hit by a van.
Rogan, Brodie & Vinnie: Noel treats Boston Terrier Rogan's debilitating dysplasia and creates an implant for Hungarian Vizsla Brodie, while a cat has leg injuries after being hit by a car.
The paramedics treat a young mum with severe stomach pains, take a breathless pensioner to hospital and attend an elderly lady with dementia whose nose will not stop bleeding.
Dudley crews attend a man with heart failure and sepsis, as well as a transgender patient who's in a lot of pain from an old injury. In Stoke, a patient has a panic attack in the ambulance.
Dr Fox is in the visitors' room for a change when his fiancee Sally has an operation to remove her wisdom teeth. Another patient has surgery to have a double hernia repaired.
One patient is flown home from holiday for an urgent operation to repair a detached retina, while another has a leaky vein that has caused an ulcer in his leg.
Not Everything Is Butterflies and Rainbows: The preceptorship midwives undergo bereavement training, while Aimee helps a family who have lost their baby.
A 21-year-old woman is rushed to Emergency after being kicked in the face by a horse and nurse Hannah comforts an elderly man who has suffered a stroke.
Documentary following Louis Theroux as he travels to San Francisco where a group of pioneering medical professionals are helping children with gender dysphoria. Ep22

programmes.show_name: Louis Theroux: Law and Disorder in Johannesburg
programmes.show_description: Louis Theroux travels to Johannesburg, where crime-plagued residents - despairing of the police - have turned to an industry of private security offering protection at a price.
Three contestants hope to walk away with a fortune, guided by seven celebrities. Can Strictly's Shirley Ballas, DJ Jo Whiley and naturalist Chris Packham help them win big?
Shopping from Home.
Today, Fresh Is Best! The Top 5 have 60 minutes to create a dish using Perino tomatoes and at least two ingredients from the MasterChef garden.
Ben Shephard hosts the quiz show in which four players take on an extraordinary machine in the hope of winning its 10,000 pound jackpot.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien look for valuable items languishing in people's homes, unearthing a church pew, create a fish tank from scrap and uncover a special petrol tank.
Longframlington: The team head to Northumberland to finish a build they started before the pandemic, helping a couple whose daughter has complex medical and mental health issues.
Stoke: The team help build a community centre and garden in Stoke-on-Trent, part of a charity set up by a local family to tackle poverty, isolation and mental health problems.

Gylly, Albert & Barney: A Pug and Beagle cross is hit by a bus, crushing an ankle and causing extensive damage to his leg, while a Labrador puppy has developed lameness.
Zizi, Suki & Raffa: We meet a Golden Retriever Poodle cross with painful elbows, a Labrador with constant pain in his front legs and Dachshund who lost the use of her back legs overnight.
Southmead: The team set to work on an adventure playground in Southmead, Bristol, which has fallen into disrepair and is now unfit for purpose.
Longframlington: The team head to Northumberland to finish a build they started before the pandemic, helping a couple whose daughter has complex medical and mental health issues.
Ren, Dave & Tallulah: A six-year-old Terrier arrives after being attacked by another dog, while Noel makes a troubling discovery with a French bulldog puppy.
Bertie, Jade & Sophie: Lively Labrador pup Bertie has gone lame, despite his youth, while Springer Spaniel Sophie has lost her bounce with a nasty kneecap problem.
Scrumpy, Buster & Ralph: Clumber Spaniel Scrumpy needs a MRI scan on his back, Dachshund Ralph is having trouble moving, and Briard Buster returns seven years after surgery.
